nodes.label: Product Purpose: Close Warm Leads via Credibility
nodes.rationale: The site exists to close leads that originate elsewhere — referrals and social drive warm traffic — so success is credibility: the portfolio convinces, services show fit, and contact/booking make the next step easy; the /admin dashboard stays functional but explicitly secondary.

nodes.label: Primary CTA: View Portfolio
nodes.rationale: Lead visitors into the work first because the work does the selling; 'Get in touch / Book a session' is the secondary, low-pressure next step once convinced.

nodes.label: Belief Ladder (beautiful work → fits my event → reliable → easy to reach)
nodes.rationale: Four beliefs the site must build in order: the work is genuinely beautiful (portfolio), they can handle MY kind of event (breadth), they're reliable professionals (process/testimonials), and reaching out is easy and low-pressure (clear contact, transparent packages).

nodes.label: Anti-Reference: Generic Template Look
nodes.rationale: Interchangeable agency/SaaS landing page — stock sections, icon grids, could be anyone's site — banned because a studio selling taste loses the sale at the aesthetic layer.

nodes.label: Anti-Reference: Cheesy Wedding-Vendor
nodes.rationale: Script fonts, rose gold, heart motifs, sentimental clip-art aesthetics — the opposite of the calm editorial register.

nodes.label: Anti-Reference: Dark Moody Luxury
nodes.rationale: Near-black backgrounds, gold serif, exclusive-hotel vibes — ink is for text and small fills, never whole viewports.

nodes.label: Anti-Reference: Trendy / Maximalist
nodes.rationale: Loud gradients, oversized type experiments, brutalist or acid aesthetics that date quickly — if it would date a printed album, it dates this site.

nodes.label: Design Principle 1: The Work Does the Talking
nodes.rationale: Layout, chrome, and copy defer to the photography — the site is the portfolio's frame, never its competitor.

nodes.label: Design Principle 2: The Site Is Itself the Proof
nodes.rationale: A studio selling taste must demonstrate taste — every detail of the site's craft is a credibility signal for a design-literate audience.

nodes.label: Design Principle 3: Calm Confidence, Never Salesmanship
nodes.rationale: No urgency tricks, no hard sells — unhurried pacing and transparent packages are the persuasion.

nodes.label: Design Principle 4: Breadth Without Dilution
nodes.rationale: Show range across weddings, corporate, concerts, and portraits while keeping one coherent voice, because no single client type dominates.

nodes.label: Design Principle 5: Never Fake the Proof (placeholder testimonials/stats)
nodes.rationale: Current testimonials, review counts ('5.0 across 180+ reviews') and stats are scaffold copy; real proof must be collected into .impeccable/assets/proof/ and swapped in before launch — invented proof must never ship.

nodes.label: WCAG 2.2 AA Commitment
nodes.rationale: At least 4.5:1 text contrast (3:1 large text), full keyboard navigation, visible focus states, and prefers-reduced-motion alternatives for all animation via the global rule in app/globals.css.

edges.source_location: Conversion & proof (belief 1: portfolio does the talking)
Design Principles (belief 2: 'they can handle MY kind of event')
Design Principles (belief 4: reaching out is easy and low-pressure)
Conversion & proof (belief 3 relies on testimonials that are currently scaffold)
Product Purpose (admin dashboard is explicitly secondary)
Conversion & proof (placeholder testimonials/stats live in lib/content.ts)
